steps.title: Spot the closing em dash for the appositive
steps.content: An em dash appears before reshuffled after the cellular organs that generate energy from light. This signals that the phrase is an appositive being set off by em dashes.

steps.title: Match the opening punctuation
steps.content:
When an appositive is set off by an em dash on one side, it must have a matching em dash on the other.

Choice D with chloroplasts— creates this matching pair to properly frame the appositive phrase.

A semicolon (A) is for independent clauses, not appositives; no punctuation (B) fails to mark the appositive; a comma (C) mismatches with the closing em dash.

That matches choice D — D is correct.
